title: Joby's 2024 Results Show Strong Cash Position as It Heads Towards Certification
text: Joby Aviation reported its 2024 financial performance on February 26, 2025, revealing a company navigating significant development costs while maintaining a surprisingly resilient cash reserve. The California-based eVTOL developer posted a full-year net loss of $608 million, with the fourth quarter alone accounting for $246.3 million of that figure. The company attributed these substantial losses to increased operational spending tied to aircraft certification efforts and manufacturing expansion. Despite ongoing cash burn, Joby ended 2024 with $932.9 million in cash, cash equivalents and liquid securities, marking a slight decrease from the prior year's $1.03 billion. The company sustained this financial cushion through more than $350 million in public share offerings during 2024, supplemented by Toyota Ventures' $500 million investment commitment. Joby projects burning between $500 to $540 million in cash during 2025—a critical year for the industry. The company anticipates achieving several milestones including FAA certification completion, serial production launch at its Ohio facility, and expanded Department of Defense testing. CEO JoeBen Bevirt characterized 2025 as a critical inflection point for both Joby and the broader eVTOL sector.
